query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer: Uganda's Bundibugyo Ebola case count remains at **20 confirmed cases** — it has **not** risen above 20. The last confirmed Uganda case was reported on **21 June 2026**, and no new cases have been reported since. This is confirmed by the ECDC outbreak page (data as of 1 July 2026, page updated 3 July 2026), which reports 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths, and 15 recoveries, and corroborated by the ebola.fyi tracker (20c/2d) and Wikipedia (2 Uganda deaths). WHO DON608 (data as of 18 June) had reported 19 Uganda cases; the single additional case around 21 June brought the total to its current 20. Meanwhile, the DRC side of the outbreak continues to grow rapidly (~900–1,460 cases depending on source/date).

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 1 July 2026, a total of 20 confirmed cases, including two deaths, reported in Uganda. The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then. Page last updated 3 July at 16:45.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON608)
sources.snippet: Published 19 June 2026 (data as of 18 June): 915 confirmed cases total; 896 DRC and 19 Uganda; 234 deaths including two from Uganda. Uganda's last confirmed case identified 5 June 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (DON605)
sources.snippet: 134 confirmed cases (nine in Uganda), including 18 deaths (one in Uganda) at time of that earlier DON.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: There have been 452 confirmed deaths in D.R. Congo, and two deaths in Uganda as of the first week of July. Bundibugyo ebolavirus; PHEIC declared 16 May 2026.

sources.title: Ebola Outbreak: Current Situation | CDC
sources.snippet: CDC responding to outbreak of Ebola caused by Bundibugyo virus in remote areas of DRC and Uganda; no US cases; risk to American public low.

key_facts: Uganda's confirmed Bundibugyo Ebola case count is 20 as of 1 July 2026 (ECDC page updated 3 July 2026).
No new confirmed cases have been reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026; the last confirmed case was reported on 21 June 2026.
Uganda's total has NOT risen above 20 confirmed cases.
Uganda has recorded 2 deaths and 15 recoveries among its 20 confirmed cases (ECDC, as of 1 July 2026).
Of Uganda's 20 cases, 15 had traveled to the DRC (imported) and 5 were linked to local transmission.

WHO DON608 (published 19 June 2026, data as of 18 June) reported 19 confirmed Uganda cases and 2 deaths, with the last case then identified 5 June; one additional case ~21 June brought the total to 20.
The outbreak (Bundibugyo virus) was declared a PHEIC by WHO on 16-17 May 2026.
In contrast to Uganda's stable count, the DRC side of the outbreak continues to grow rapidly (roughly 900 to 1,460 cases depending on source and date).
Multiple sources corroborate Uganda = 20 cases / 2 deaths: ECDC outbreak page, ebola.fyi tracker, and Wikipedia.

data_points.metric: DRC total reported cases (suspected+confirmed, DRC SitRep via ECDC)
